text.title: django-service-layer Core Rules
text.summary: Five testable rules for HackSoft-style Django services. Each is checkable via AST inspection or pytest with `transaction.TestCase`.

rule.statement: Service functions MUST take keyword-only arguments (`def _(*, ...)`). List of business inputs must be exhaustive — no `**kwargs` passthrough.
rule.rationale: Keyword-only signatures self-document call sites and prevent argument-order regressions when fields are added. `**kwargs` passthrough silently swallows typos.

rule.statement: A service that writes to more than one model row (any combination of create/update/delete across one or several models) MUST wrap the writes in a single `transaction.atomic()` block at the service body. The boundary lives in the service, not in the view, not in middleware, not implicitly via `ATOMIC_REQUESTS=True`.
rule.rationale: Without an explicit boundary, partial failure leaves the database in an inconsistent state. ATOMIC_REQUESTS wraps the entire view in one transaction, which mixes business logic and HTTP plumbing into one rollback unit — long transactions, lock contention, and cross-service nesting headaches.

rule.statement: Service code that constructs a new model instance MUST call `instance.full_clean()` before `instance.save()`. Forbid `Model.objects.create(...)` shortcut for instances whose model defines a `clean()` method or constraints not enforceable by Django field validators alone.
rule.rationale: `objects.create` skips `clean()`. Multi-field invariants defined in `clean()` (e.g. `total = sum(items.price)`) never fire, allowing invalid rows. `full_clean()` is the only call that runs field validators + clean() together.

rule.statement: Services MUST raise domain exceptions (defined in `core/exceptions.py`: `ApplicationError`, `NotFoundError`, `ValidationError`, `PermissionDeniedError`). Forbid `rest_framework.exceptions.*` and `django.http.Http404` in services. The view (or DRF exception handler) is responsible for mapping domain → HTTP.
rule.rationale: DRF / HTTP exceptions in services prevent reuse from Celery tasks and management commands (which have no HTTP layer). The mapping layer is a single function that converts domain → HTTP and lives next to the API surface.

rule.statement: Side effects (Celery `.delay()`, email send, webhook dispatch, cache invalidation) MUST be wrapped in `transaction.on_commit(lambda: ...)`. They MUST NOT execute inside the `atomic()` block unconditionally.
rule.rationale: Tasks dispatched inside an atomic block may execute on the worker before the transaction commits, seeing stale state. on_commit defers execution until after commit (and skips entirely on rollback).
